## 3. CHANGED â€” endpoint exists, but method / auth / transport / response shape diverges (18)

### 3.1 â€” `GET /api/auth/me` non-standard response envelope (6)

> Backend returns raw `{status:"success", data:UserResource}` (uses `response()->json`, NOT the standard `ApiResponse` envelope). The axios interceptor at `frontend/lib/axios.ts` (response handler) returns `response.data.data as T` â€” so the unwrap target is wrong: the call site will receive `{status:"success", ...}` instead of the `UserResource`. Either:
> (a) the interceptor must special-case `/auth/me` and unwrap the inner `data` field, or
> (b) the frontend should call `api.get<{status, data:UserResource}>(...)` and pull `.data` explicitly.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Correct target contract |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/admin/center-codes/page.tsx:90` | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | as above |
| 2 | `app/admin/page.tsx:85` | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | as above |
| 3 | `app/admin/plan/page.tsx:41` | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | as above |
| 4 | `app/components/Navbar.tsx:36` | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | as above |
| 5 | `app/dashboard/page.tsx:117` | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | as above |
| 6 | `app/page.tsx:138` | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | as above |

### 3.2 â€” `GET /api/exams/my-results` non-standard response envelope (1)

> Backend returns raw `{data:[â€¦]}` â€” no `success`, no `message`. The axios interceptor returns `response.data.data` which will yield `undefined` (since there is no nested `data`). Frontend must read `response.data.data` itself, **or** the page must adapt the response.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Correct target contract |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/dashboard/page.tsx:160` | `GET` | `/api/exams/my-results` | `{data:[{id, lectureId, courseTitle, lectureTitle, formIndex, score, passed, completedAt}]}` raw; read `.data` directly. |

### 3.3 â€” `DELETE /api/admin/questions/{id}` from the comprehensive-exams page routes to the WRONG handler (1)

> The same URL is registered twice (regular exam #117 + comprehensive exam #125). The comprehensive controller method `destroyQuestion` is **DEAD**, so Laravel resolves to the regular-exam `Question` delete handler. Calling it from the comprehensive-exams page will delete a regular exam question with the same id (if it exists) instead of the intended comprehensive one. The frontend call is correctly aimed at the comprehensive surface but the URL is shared with the regular surface and the wrong one wins.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/admin/courses/[id]/comprehensive-exams/page.tsx:355` | `DELETE` | `/api/admin/questions/{qId}` | Switch to a dedicated URL (e.g. `DELETE /api/admin/comprehensive-exams/questions/{id}`) once the backend adds the route, or stop using the page's "delete question" affordance until a fix ships. |

### 3.4 â€” Wrong HTTP verb: PUT used where backend expects PATCH (2)

> The backend exposes PATCH for partial updates. The ported frontend uses `fetch(..., { method: 'PUT' })`. Laravel's route binding will route PUT requests differently from PATCH for routes registered as `Route::patch(...)`, and the frontend call will 405/404.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Correct verb |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/admin/courses/page.tsx:96` (edit branch) | `PUT` | `/api/admin/courses/{course}` (#92) | `PATCH` |
| 2 | `app/admin/courses/[id]/lectures/page.tsx:209` (edit branch) | `PUT` | `/api/admin/lectures/{lecture}` (#98) | `PATCH` |

### 3.5 â€” Missing required path parameter (1)

> Backend route binding requires `{user}` in the URL. The frontend call omits it.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Correct path |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/admin/exams/page.tsx:168` | `POST` | `/api/admin/lectures/{lectureId}/reset-attempts` | `/api/admin/lectures/{lecture}/reset-attempts/{user}` (#110) â€” pass the row's `userId` from `ExamAttempt` (the `results` payload includes `userId` in #108). |

### 3.6 â€” Wrong transport for parameter (1)

> Backend expects `{provider}` in the request **body**. The frontend puts it in the **query string** and sends an empty body.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/wallet/page.tsx:204` | `POST` | `/api/wallet/topup/initiate?provider=${method}` | Send `Content-Type: application/json` body `{provider: "instapay" \| "vodafone_cash"}` to the same URL (#48). |

### 3.7 â€” Wrong path: `/api/student/center-codes/redeem` does not exist (1)

> The student redeem route is at `/api/center-codes/redeem` (no `/student/` segment). The frontend is adding an extra prefix.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Correct path |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/redeem/page.tsx:69` | `POST` | `/api/student/center-codes/redeem` | `/api/center-codes/redeem` (#50) â€” body unchanged: `{code:"XXXX-XXXX-XXXX"}` |

### 3.8 â€” Login body missing required `device_id` (1)

> Backend login requires a third parameter, `device_id`, for the single-session Sanctum invariant. Without it the request 422s.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/login/page.tsx:62` | `POST` | `/api/auth/login` (#7) | Add `device_id` to the JSON body (e.g. `crypto.randomUUID()` or a stable per-install id stored in `localStorage`). |

### 3.9 â€” Verify-OTP body uses `otp` instead of `firebase_token` (1)

> Backend requires `{temp_user_id, firebase_token}` â€” the value is the **Firebase phone-auth ID token** obtained from the Firebase JS SDK (`firebase/auth` â†’ `signInWithPhoneNumber` â†’ `confirmationResult.confirm(code)` â†’ `user.getIdToken()`), **not** the 6-digit OTP code.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/otp/page.tsx:104` | `POST` | `/api/auth/verify-otp` (#8) | Body must be `{temp_user_id, firebase_token}`; integrate Firebase phone auth on the client and send the ID token. |

### 3.10 â€” Exam submit body shape is wrong (1)

> Backend body is `{exam_id:int, answers:{question_id: selected_answer}}` (object map). Frontend sends `{attempt_id, answers:[{question_id, answer}]}` (array of objects, plus a non-existent `attempt_id` field).

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/exams/[lectureId]/page.tsx:269` | `POST` | `/api/lectures/{lecture}/exam/{exam}/submit` (#30) | Body: `{exam_id:<int>, answers:{ "<qid>": <selectedOptionIndex>, ... }}` |

### 3.11 â€” Comprehensive exam purchase response fields are snake_case (1)

> Backend returns `{comprehensive_exam_id, amount_paid, new_balance, wallet_transaction_id}` (legacy holdover â€” see inventory Â§32.3). Frontend must read snake_case (or both casings defensively).

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/comprehensive-exams/[id]/page.tsx:111` | `POST` | `/api/comprehensive-exams/{id}/purchase` (#33) | Read `data.comprehensive_exam_id / data.amount_paid / data.new_balance / data.wallet_transaction_id` (or both casings). |

### 3.12 â€” Admin lecture attachment upload response uses `file_url` (snake_case) (1)

> Legacy holdover â€” see inventory Â§32.3.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/admin/courses/[id]/lectures/page.tsx:341` | `POST` | `/api/admin/lectures/{lecture}/attachments` (#104) | Read `data.file_url` (snake) â€” do not assume camelCase. |

### 3.13 â€” Wallet transactions response has `balance_After` (snake_case) (1)

> Legacy holdover â€” see inventory Â§32.3. Read `balanceBefore` (camel) **and** `balance_After` (snake) defensively.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Fix |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `app/wallet/page.tsx:123` | `GET` | `/api/wallet/transactions?limit=10` (#46) | Read both `balanceBefore` and `balance_After`. |

### 3.14 â€” `/api/auth/me` in the e2e test loop (1)

> Same envelope issue as Â§3.1 â€” but the test only asserts the HTTP status, so the test itself passes; it is logged here for completeness.

| # | File:Line | Method | Call site target | Correct target contract |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `e2e/security.spec.ts:25` (loop iteration) | `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | non-standard `{status:"success", data:UserResource}` |

## 7. Sanity notes (carried forward from the inventory, not new findings)

These are global invariants the rewire phase should apply uniformly while fixing the items above â€” they are not separate call-site mismatches, but they explain **why** so many call sites break at once.

- **Single active Sanctum session** â€” every successful login wipes all of the user's prior tokens, and so do `POST /admin/users/{user}/reset-password`, `POST /admin/security/block-student/{user}`, and 3-fatal-strike auto-block. Any 401 after a brief lull = redirect to login; never retry with a stale token.
- **`active_user` middleware is the kill switch** â€” a student blocked in the admin UI gets 403 on every protected route, instantly, even mid-session.
- **B2 signed URLs are short-lived** â€” 1 h for HLS resource, 5 min for `.ts`/student images/homework, 10 min for admin file URLs. Never persist them in `localStorage`/cookies; re-fetch on demand.
- **/api/metrics hides itself** â€” non-allowlisted IPs get 404, not 403. The frontend should never 404-handle a metrics path; treat it as missing.
- **Response envelope is `{success, message, data}` everywhere** â€” except 5 documented legacy holdovers (see inventory Â§32.3): `/auth/me`, `/exams/my-results`, `WalletTransactionResource.balance_After`, `StudentComprehensiveExamController.*` (snake_case throughout), `AdminCenterCodeController::export` (snake_case), `AdminLectureController` attachment upload `file_url` (snake_case).

---

*End of contract diff. The rewire phase should treat Â§1 and Â§2 as must-fix blockers and Â§3 as required fixes; Â§4 confirms there is no UI gap to remediate.*
